license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.57cg9iw
title: LAMP in situ complete
author: Nicholas W. West

steps_raw.instruction: Harvest tissue and submerge in an excess of FAA solution, pull a vacuum for ~2 min and agitate samples gently. Hold under vacuum for 2-3 min before slowly releasing vacuum. Once the vacuum is released the samples should sink, if tissue samples float to the surface, agitate gently and repeat vacuum step if necessary.
2 washes in 50% EtOH, 5 min per wash
Incubate samples at 4 °C for 10 to 14 hours
2 washes in 50% EtOH, 30 min per wash
Incubate 10 min in 75% EtOH at 60 °C
Can be held in 75% EtOH overnight at 4 °C
Incubate 10 min 85% EtOH at 60 °C
Incubate 10 min 95% EtOH at 60 °C
2 washes in 100% EtOH, 10 min per wash at 60 °C
Incubate 10 min 3:1 EtOH (100%) : Histoclear (or xylenes) at 60 °C
Incubate 10 min 1:1 EtOH (100%) : Histoclear (or xylenes) at 60 °C
Incubate 10 min 1:3 EtOH (100%) : Histoclear (or xylenes) at 60 °C
Incubate 5 min Histoclear (or xylenes) at 60 °C
Incubate 10 min 1:1 Histoclear (or xylenes) : Paraffin at 60 °C
5 washes in paraffin at 60 °C 30 min per wash
Can be held overnight in paraffin
Place tissue molds and tweezers/tools in oven (or preffered heating element)

Transfer tissue into pre-warmed tissue molds, fill molds with paraffin and align samples into desired orientation. Wait for bubbles to disperse, accelerate dispersal with gentle agitation if desired. Remove molds from oven and allow to cool

Tissue sectionsing is a skill and a well angeled and extremely sharp blade is critical. An excellent guide to the technique can be found within "Plant Mircotechnique and Microscopy" by Steven Ruzin 1999

Use the initial sections to adjust the microtome such that sections ribbon together. When approaching the perceived area of interest reduce the number of sections per slide. Float section ribbon on 45 °C diH2O, capture on slide leaving a wide margin around sample.
Bake tissue sections overnight at ~ 45 °C
Incubate slides in Histoclear (or xylenes) for 10 min at 55 °C
3 washes of Histoclear (or xylenes) at 55 °C for 5 min per wash
3 washes of 100% EtOH at 55 °C for 2 min per wash
Incubate 2 min in 95% EtOH
Incubate 2 min in 70% EtOH
Incubate 2 min in 50% EtOH
Incubate 2 min in DEPC H2O
Incubate 2 min in 1xTBS
Can be held overnight in 1xTBS
Prepare Proteinase K buffer (1xTE, 0.5% Triton-x-100, 20ug/uL Proteinase K), vortex before adding Proteinase K.
Remove slide, drain excess buffer, and outline tissue with hydrophobic pen/wax pencil
Add 250 µL Proteinase K buffer to each slide and place in humidity chamber at 37 °C for 20 min
Wash 3 times with 1xTBS for 5 min per wash
Detecting DNA? Proceed to LAMP Reaction Detecting RNA? Remove genomic background first
Can be held overnight at 4 °C
Prepare a 40 µL DNase I reaction per slide according to manufacturers (Thermo Scientific) instructions
Prewarm humidity chamber to 37 °C
Apply DNase I reaction mix drop-wise onto tissue sections, pitch and roll slide to ensure the reaction covers all tissue samples. Place slide into humidity chamber and incubate at 37 °C for 1 hour
To prevent the reaction mix from pooling and encourage more uniform coverage of tissue samples, every 5-7 min gently pitch and roll the humidity chamber
Wash 3 times in 1xTBS, 5 min per wash, proceed to LAMP reaction
Can be held overnight at 4 °C
Prewarm humidity chamber to 65 °C
Prepare a 30 µL LAMP reaction per slide as outlined in the manufacturers (New England Biosciences) instructions.

Apply desired LAMP reaction (colorimeteric or fluorescent) mix drop-wise to the tissue samples, pitch and roll the slide to encourage uniform distribution. Place in humidity chamber and incubate for an empirically determined amount of time, typically between 30 min to 1 hour
To avoid pooling and encourage even distribution of the reaction mix, every 5-7 min gently pitch and roll the humidity chamber for the duration of incubation
2 washes in 1xMAB, 5 min per wash, proceed to detection
Incubate slides in 1xBlocking buffer with gentle rocking at room temperature for 1 hour
Flood slide with 250 µL of a 1:5000 anti-DIG alkaline phosphatase conjugated antibody in 1xBlocking buffer, place in humidity chamber and incubate at room temperature for 1 hour
Wash 5 times in 1xMAB, 5 min per wash
Flood slides with 250 µL NBT/BCIP, incubate at room temperature until reaction develops to satisfactory levels, typically 20-30 min
Stop reaction by flooding slides with diH2O, mount a slide cover with your prefered method
Can be held overnight at 4 °C
For colorimeteric detection add 0.15 µL of digoxigenin-11-dUTP, 25nmol (25uL) from Roche, this replaces a portion of the H2O in the reaction mix
For fluorescent detection use fluorescently labeled loop primer mixture in the standard LAMP reaction
For fluorescent detection procced to step 43
